component_identification.content: Components: 1 game board, 7 role cards, 7 pawns, 6 research stations, 96 disease cubes (24 each in 4 colors), 48 infection cards, 59 player cards (48 city cards, 5 event cards, 6 epidemic cards), 4 cure markers, 1 infection-rate marker, 1 outbreaks marker, and rulebook/reference cards.

core_game_loop.content: Each player turn follows strict order: take up to 4 actions, draw 2 player cards, then infect cities based on the current infection rate. The team collaborates to discover all 4 cures before a loss condition occurs. Roles bend rules and define coordination priorities, but turn sequence never changes.

detailed_rules.content: Actions include Drive/Ferry, Direct Flight, Charter Flight, Shuttle Flight, Build Research Station, Treat Disease, Share Knowledge, and Discover Cure. Treat removes 1 cube (all cubes of that color if cured; Medic has stronger effect). To discover a cure, be at a research station and discard 5 same-color city cards (Scientist needs 4). Epidemic cards do 3 steps: increase infection rate, infect bottom card city with 3 cubes, then intensify by shuffling infection discard pile onto top of deck. Outbreaks happen when placing a 4th cube of a color in a city; add one cube to each connected city and advance outbreak track. During one infection step, a city can outbreak only once, but chain reactions can propagate. If a color supply is empty when cubes must be placed, team loses immediately. Dispatcher/Operations Expert/Quarantine Specialist and events create timing edge cases; event cards may be played at almost any time as written, even on other players’ turns.

scoring_and_endgame.content: Pandemic is win/lose, not VP. Team wins instantly when all four cures are discovered. Team loses if outbreaks marker reaches 8, if any disease cube color cannot be placed, or if players cannot draw required player cards because the deck is empty.

beginner_strategy.content: 1) Early game priority is board stability—prevent double/triple-cube clusters from chaining. 2) Build a station network for mobility, not everywhere; 3–4 well-placed stations is usually enough. 3) Plan card routing two turns ahead so cures complete efficiently. 4) Spend events to prevent catastrophe, not minor inconvenience. 5) Once a disease is cured, aggressively clear that color to reduce infection pressure.

metadata.notes: Check 4 failed: total_token_count is 458; must be 800-3000 (hard reject if <800 or >5000)
